texts.full_text: add some time (the trip to los Gatos was only 4.25 hours long, much to my dismay). It was a definite stretch – I was feeling pretty tired near the end, but my legs and my butt held out, and I’m not feeling any tenderness in either knee or medial quad muscle, so I’m fairly well satisfied. Assuming things go well tomorrow and in the nExt week, I think I will essay Day on the Ride next weekend, at 82 miles. I wouldn’t do it except that the route will have excellent support, so if I need to sag or sweep I can do it. Error { "code": "BadRequest", "message": "{\"Message\":\"The request is invalid.\",\"ModelState\":{\"variable.properties.value\":[\"An error has occurred.\"]}}" } Template { "name": "mystring", "type": "variables", "apiVersion": "2015-10-31", "dependsOn": [ "[concat('microsoft.Automation/automationAccounts/', parameters('AutomationAccountName'))]" ], "properties": { "value": { "reference": { "keyVault": { "id": "[resourceId(subscription().subscriptionId, 'Utility-RG', 'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', 'MyKeyVault')]" It seems you are facing an issue with using the Azure Key Vault secret in a linked template to create an automation variable for your Windows passWord retrieval process. Based on the provided error message: ```json { "code": "BadRequest", "message": "{\"Message\":\"The request is invalid.\",\"ModelState\":{\"variable.properties.value\":[\"An error has occurred.\"]}}" } ``` It indicates that there might be an issue with your template's structure or the way you are referencing the Key Vault secret within it, leading to a "BadRequest" response from Azure Automation service. Here is how I would approach troubleshooting this: 1. Review template Structure: Ensure that all dependencies and references in your linked templ

texts.full_text: ing completely honest with them about who I am and what I feel in my heart and soul.” – J. Serrato Biography Joseph Serrato grew up knowing he would be a musician. He wanted to follow his father’s footstepsand become an accomplished guitarist, just like him. Joseph's passion for music was evident from a young age as he spent countless hours practicing and honing his craft. Born in San Antonio, Texas, into a family that valued tradition and hard work, Serrato found solace and expression through the strings of his guitar. His father, an accomplished musician himself, recognized this talent early on and encouraged Joseph to pursue music professionally. With unwavering support from his parents, he began performing at local venues whIle still in high school. After graduating with honors, Joseph decided it was time to take a leap into the professional world. He moved to Austin, Texas—the heart of American live music—to immerse himself fully in its vibrant scene. It wasn't long before Serrato started making waves; his unique blend of blues and rock resonated deeply with audiences. Joseph's dedication soon led him to form "Soul Strings," a band that quickly gained popularity for their electrifying performances and heartfelt lyrics. Together, they released several albums which were critically acclaimed and earned them numerous awards in the music industry. Despite achieving fame, Joseph remained grounded by his roots and continued writing songs inspired by personal experiences and emotions. His authenticity struck a chord with fans worldwide who appreciated him not just as an artist but also for his genuine connection to life's ups and downs. Today, J. Serrato is celebrated both on stage and in the studio, where he continues to create music that touches hearts across generations. He remains committed to sha
